units.title: Health and Safety in Carpentry and Joinery Work Environment

learning_outcomes.description: Know work practices and instructions in a Work environment

performance_criteria.description: Explain safe work practices and instructions to Assistant Carpenter.
Compare safe work practices and instructions in accordance with company safety guides.
Apply safe work practices to a given task such as formwork, scaffolding, etc.
Follow safe methods and procedures of work practices.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand Safety hazards and risks in the work Environment.

performance_criteria.description: Recognize safety signs and symbols in work environment
Identify potential hazards and risks in work environment.
Explain potential hazards and risks in surface, heights and depth areas.
Take appropriate actions to prevent accident/injury.
Carry out safety drills.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the use of various types of PPE in the work environment.

performance_criteria.description: Identify types of PPE and their uses.
Select and wear appropriate PPE correctly.
Provide PPE to Assistant Carpenter and supervise him/her to follow instructions.
Maintain and return PPE for storage.
Apply appropriate methods of lifting techniques for materials in work environment.
Implement proper stacking methods and procedures in material handling.
Guide on proper application of manual lifting techniques for heavy materials.
Check on application of safe work practices and procedures in work environment.
Provide health and safety information of organizational safety policy to Assistant Carpenter.
Explain the importance of health and safety to Assistant Carpenter.

Compare Assistant Carpenter work practices with safety guidelines Unit Purpose To establish a quality communication system that is responsive and subject to change in meeting workers and employers need, in work Environment. Unit assessment evidence: To perform effectively and efficiently, the learner must show evidence requirements through: Observation Question and answers Witness testimony Work product Personal statement/Reflective account Objectives: By completing this mandatory unit, learner must show competence in: 1. Using a non-complex communication system in a work environment. 2. Developing the ability to identify the source of information in a work environment. 3. Recognize various communication means in a work environment. 4. Maintaining and use communication equipment. Ref. Page Number

learning_outcomes.description: Complex communication system in a work environment.

performance_criteria.description: Supervise the use of audio, electronic and Electro-magnetic means to pass on necessary information.
Describe simple non-verbal means of communication.
Read and interpret concept of symbols and signs appropriately.

learning_outcomes.description: Source of Information in a work environment.

performance_criteria.description: Participate in creating and making functional the source of information in an organization, and work environment.
Make accessible the source of information in an organization or work environment.
Relate appropriately with the Source of information.
Use the various information flow system in an organization or work environment to overcome challenges.
Ensure proper documentation and retrieval of information in accordance with procedure in a work environment.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand various communication means in a work environment

performance_criteria.description: Ensure the accessibility of the communication equipment in the work environment.
Describe the effective use of the Various communication channels in a work environment.
Demonstrate the use of various communication means in a work environment.
Ensure effective information flow to the right personnel.
Ensure the effective use of symbols, signs and codes.
Ensure that instructions are obeyed and disseminated in line with ethics of the work environment.
Ensure that communication equipment are in good working conditions.
Liaise with the maintenance unit to ensure that communication equipment are maintained regularly.
Liaise with appropriate authority to replace communication equipment in the event of loss or damage.
Ensure that communication equipment are stored appropriately in a work environment.

learning_outcomes.description: Know positive working relationship with colleagues.

performance_criteria.description: Identify the need for developing positive working relationship with colleagues.
Recognize the importance of relating with other people in a way that makes them feel valued and respected.
Assist team members when required.
Report to the appropriate personnel when request for assistance falls outside area of your area responsibility.
Communicate information to colleagues about own work that might affect others.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand how to take responsibilities within the team.

performance_criteria.description: Recognize own role and responsibilities within the team.
Perform individual tasks in line with the team rules and regulations
Participate effectively in teamwork.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand compliance with policy of organization.

performance_criteria.description: Work in line with the policy of organizational standard.
Use organization code of practice.
Explain organizational code of conduct.

learning_outcomes.description: Know how to use hand tools and equipment in carpentry joinery work.

performance_criteria.description: Select hand tools for cutting and planing.
Use hand tools correctly and efficiently following safe methods and procedures for marking, cutting etc. in accordance with instructions.
Maintain all hand tools used in accordance with manufacturer’s instructions.
Clean and return hand tools used for proper storage.
Identify the various Portable Power Tools (PPT) and equipment such as orbital sanders, portable power planer, portable power drill, portable power jig saw etc.

learning_outcomes.description: Know how to use portable power tools in Carpentry and Joinery work.

performance_criteria.description: Describe and explain the uses of PPT in 1.1 above.
Select and safely use appropriate PPT according to a given task such as formwork, scaffold etc.
Maintain all PPT used following manufacturer’s guidelines and instructions.
Clean and return all PPT used for proper storage.
Identify the various fixed woodworking machines such as spindle moulder, thicknesser, band saws, drilling, cross- cutting machines, circular saw etc.
Select appropriate machine for specific task such as drilling.
Check to ensure that the machine is in good condition.
Operate drilling machine to perform a given task such as drilling, boring etc. safely.
Maintain equipment in accordance with instruction.
Carryout proper housekeeping.

learning_outcomes.description: Know the Health and safety requirements in handling materials and storage.

performance_criteria.description: Source information in work environment
Identify sources of obtaining materials.
Observe safety in loading and off- loading of materials.
Observe safety in handling materials.
Observe safety in using materials.
Observe safety in returning of unused materials for storage.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate ability to select from range of materials used in carpentry and joinery.

performance_criteria.description: Differentiate between soft and hardwood.
Explain timber processing methods such as felling, logging, conversion, seasoning etc.
Describe timber seasoning and preservation.
Identify various timber defects.
Identify causes of timber defects.
Adopt appropriate methods of storing timber.

learning_outcomes.description: Know the various types of manufactured boards and storage methods.

performance_criteria.description: Identify various types of Manufactured boards such as plywood, particles board, hardboards, medium density fiber boards etc.
Identify various sizes of Manufactured boards.
Select Manufactured boards for specific task in carpentry and joinery.
Adopt an appropriate method of storing Manufactured boards.
Select bonding materials in relation to Manufacture boards.
Apply bonding materials such as animal glue in accordance with manufacturer’s instructions.
Apply bonding materials in accordance with given instructions.
Clean excess bonding materials in accordance with instructions.
Return unused bonding materials for storage.
Apply fastening materials for the construction of bookshelf.
Return un-used fastening materials for storage.

learning_outcomes.description: Know how to apply wood finishes.

performance_criteria.description: Explain the use of various wood finishes such as thinner, lacquer, paraffin, polishes, paints etc.
State various reasons for the use of finishes in Carpentry and Joinery work.
Transport the selected wood finishes to work location in accordance with safety instructions.
Select appropriate methods in applying wood finishes for specific item such as bookshelf etc.
Ensure unused finishing materials are returned for proper storage.
E xplain the effect of hazardous materials such as Epoxy glue, Solignum, French polish, etc. used in carpentry and joinery work environment.
H andle hazardous materials such as Polyurethanes, Polyimides and Solignum etc. following safe methods and procedure in accordance with instructions.
Take action to protect self-using appropriate PPE.
Take action to protect others.
Identify wood chemicals such as Paraffin Wax according to their applications.
State the techniques for applying wood chemicals such as brushing, spraying, deeping, steeping etc.
Apply wood chemicals such as Paraffin Wax etc. for a given task.
Return unused finishing materials safely for proper storage.
Return unused hazardous materials safely for proper storage.

learning_outcomes.description: Information in a work environment.

performance_criteria.description: Identify portable power tools used in site clearance.
Select appropriate tools and equipment for specific sites.
Clear site observing safe work practices and procedures.
Maintain the tools and equipment.
Return tools and equipment for safe storage.

learning_outcomes.description: Excavate foundation trench.

performance_criteria.description: Identify basic tools used in setting out and excavation.
Identify the methods and procedures in setting out of the structure.
Assist in setting out observing safe work practices.
Identify and demonstrate the necessary procedures in excavation.
Erect timbering against faces of excavation.
Apply anti-termite treatment observing safe method of handling hazardous materials.
Maintain and return all tools and unused materials for safe storage.
Identify material for u sed filling.
State procedure for levelling.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand scaffolding operations.

performance_criteria.description: Differentiate between types of scaffolds (dependent, independent etc.) used in work environment.
Explain the basic requirement of when and how to use dependent and independent scaffolds.
Select different scaffolding components such as props, platforms, brace, toe board, guardrail etc.
Use safety devices such as safety belt.
Select appropriate scaffold according to task to be carried out.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the erection of scaffolds

performance_criteria.description: State procedures and method of erecting dependent and independent scaffolds.
Erect dependent and independent scaffolds to meet functional requirements.
Check scaffold for strength, rigidity and stability.
Maintain erected scaffold.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand procedure of dismantling dependent and independent scaffolds.

performance_criteria.description: Dismantle dependent and independent scaffold after use in accordance with procedure.
Carry dismantled scaffold members in accordance with loading and off-loading techniques.
Stack dismantled scaffolds members in accordance with instruction of designated/specified locations.
Carry out housekeeping and maintain clean work environment in accordance with work and safety at work regulations Act.

learning_outcomes.description: Process of constructing access to platform.

performance_criteria.description: Explain the process of constructing access to work platform.
Select material for making access to working platform.
Select appropriate tools for making access to working platform e.g. hammer, saw etc.

learning_outcomes.description: Construct access to work platform.

performance_criteria.description: Prepare material for access to working platform.
Apply safety in constructing work access platform.
Construct access to platform using the correct procedure.
Return tools for proper storage e.g. nails, solid wood etc.

learning_outcomes.description: Dismantle access to work platform.

performance_criteria.description: Explain the process of dismantling access to work platform.
Dismantle access to work platform using the correct procedures.
Stack dismantled members according to instruction.
Return tools for proper storage.
C arry out proper housekeeping

learning_outcomes.description: Understand how to select resources for formwork

performance_criteria.description: Select the relevant materials to be used for formwork operations.
Select the relevant tools to be used for the task.
Identify the hazards associated with the resources and use of tools.
Describe how to protect work, tools, materials and equipment from damage.
State how to avoid human injury.
State how to dispose waste safely.

learning_outcomes.description: Know how to erect formwork to specification within allowed time below ground level.

performance_criteria.description: Interpret work specification to meet deadline.
Interpret drawings, specifications, codes, symbols, schedules for the task.
Measure, mark-out, cut and finish work.
Use portable power tools etc.
Erect formwork below ground level of given working instructions for at least 2 of the following: • Walls • Columns • Beams (ground or suspended) • Suspended floors.
Fasten and brace the formwork.
Secure the work to health and safety requirement.
Protect formwork from damage and distortion.
Return unused material for storage.
Carry out proper housekeeping of the workplace.
Apply safe work practices in erecting formwork above ground level.
Erect formwork for columns, beams, floor, slabs, kickers, stop ends, precast units etc.
Strike formwork for columns, beams, floor, slabs, kickers, stop ends, precast units etc.
Attach fasteners, braces, soldiers and wallings to the formwork.

learning_outcomes.description: Know how to Dismantle Formwork.

performance_criteria.description: Apply safety procedures relevant to dismantling formwork.
Apply appropriate method in dismantling formwork.
Remove fasteners and braces.
Stack fasteners and braces
Remove the formwork members.
Stack the formwork members.

learning_outcomes.description: Know how to prepare timber and manufactured boards to the right sizes for a given task.

performance_criteria.description: Identify standard for timber and manufactured board.
Calculate the dimensions of table length, height and width.
Use measuring devices such as gauges, measuring tapes, ruler, try square etc.
Cut timber/manufactured board to sizes as contained in the working drawing.
Plane wood component of table members to actual sizes.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand various wood joints in Carpentry and Joinery.

performance_criteria.description: Identify various joints used in carpentry and joinery work.
Read basic wood work joint sketches.
Sketch basic wood work joints.
Identify basic assembly tools and equipment.

learning_outcomes.description: Know the procedure for constructing and assembling various wood joints.

performance_criteria.description: Prepare wood/manufactured board to construct bookshelf, drawers, stool magazine rack etc.
Construct joints such as dove tail, tongue and groove etc.
Dress joint members neatly.
Apply synthetic bond neatly to joint members.
Assemble joint members using tools such as sash clamp, mallet, hammer etc.
Clean joints neatly and keep in a safe place to set.
Identify simple basic hand drilling tools used in carpentry and joinery work in accordance with specification and measurement.
Describe different drilling procedures to include stopped hole, through hole etc. using appropriate drill sizes and procedures safely.
Use portable power drill correctly in accordance with instruction given for task such as stopped hole, through hole etc.
Use hand tools correctly in accordance with instructions given for the task.
Maintain and return used tools to appropriate storage space.

learning_outcomes.description: Access information for the desired task.

performance_criteria.description: Identify drawings, specifications, schedules etc.
Explain method statement, risk assessments, manufacturer’s information and regulation governing buildings.
Use various types of information, their sources and how they are interpreted.
Explain the organizational procedures developed to report and rectify inappropriate information and unsuitable resources and how they are implemented.

learning_outcomes.description: Safe work practices.

performance_criteria.description: Determine the level of knowledge operatives must have on relevant information, current legislative, official guidance and how it is applied.
Explain how emergencies should be reported and responded to.
Use organizational security procedures for tools, equipment, materials and personal belongings.
Identify accident reporting procedures and who is responsible for making the report.
Explain when and how health and safety control equipment should be used.
I dentify the characteristics associated with resources such as wood, tools finance, transport etc.
Explain how defects should be rectified.
Explain how the resources should be used and any problems associated with the resources are reported.
Explain procedures for selecting resources.
Identify hazards associated with the resources and methods of work and how they can be overcome.
Select good materials, components before fixing timber on walls or floors.
Use correct tools and equipment while erecting timber walls/floors.

learning_outcomes.description: Identify and use protective devices.

performance_criteria.description: Use protective devices against accident and injury.
Select materials for erecting wall and floors.
Identify the means of minimizing damage and maintain clean work space.
Dispose all identified waste in accordance with legislation.

learning_outcomes.description: Construct wooden walls and floors

performance_criteria.description: Explain the required work expertise in carrying out operations such as; measurements, marking out, fitting and aligning members in secure position.
Select good grained timber e.g. teak, afara, mansonia etc. for erecting walls/floors.
Select hand tools, portable power tools and ancillary equipment for the task.
Cut/ trim the timber to a uniform thickness to obtain good looking surfaces
Fix sole plate and other members in accordance to specification.
Select specific wood finishes for timber walls and floors.
Use allocated time for a task efficiently.
Maintain allowable time for completion of allocated work.
Discuss the allocated time in order to meet the needs of the other occupations and/or client.
M aintain completed work to prevent damages
Carry out housekeeping and return all unused materials for safe storage

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the process of installing frame, lining and side hung doors.

performance_criteria.description: Explain the process of installing a frame.
State the sequence of installing a side hung door.
State the safety requirements of installing frame, lining and side hung doors.
Select the tools required for installing a frame.

learning_outcomes.description: Know the tools requirement for installation of frames, linings and side hung doors.

performance_criteria.description: Select hand, portable power tools and equipment that are deployed in installation of framed doors and fixtures.
Apply hand tools correctly in accordance with instructions given for the installation of frames and fittings.
Return used tools to appropriate storage space.

learning_outcomes.description: Installation of frames, linings and side hung doors.

performance_criteria.description: Prepare the wall opening to receive jamb frame and lining.
Bring the right size of frame and lining to the wall opening.
Measure and ensure that the frames and lining are in conformity with specification.
Trim to appropriate size.
Fix the frame to the opening.
Plumb the frame to the opening.
Fix lining and hinges appropriately.
Protect the work from damage and distortion.
Carry out proper housekeeping.
Maintain and return all tools used for safe storage.

learning_outcomes.description: Application of the knowledge of computer appreciation.

performance_criteria.description: Identify and explain computer and its accessories.
Operate computer by starting and shutting down effectively.
Understand Microsoft office.
Use Microsoft word to create document.
Use Microsoft excel to create spread sheet.,

learning_outcomes.description: Saving and retrieving information.

performance_criteria.description: Save information.
Retrieve information whenever required.
Edit and modify information whenever required.

learning_outcomes.description: Updating and validating information using computer system.

performance_criteria.description: Update information/record appropriately.
Select and share appropriate information for communication.
Process data.

learning_outcomes.description: Identification of tools and software for power system operation.

performance_criteria.description: Identify tools and software used for power system operation in your environment.
Explain the basics of operating the tools and software.

learning_outcomes.description: Formulae and charts usage in excel.

performance_criteria.description: Explain how to use formulae in excel operation.
Explain the procedure of creating simple formulae in excel relevant to your operation.
Open and interpret charts in excel.
Use formulae and chart to represent information.

learning_outcomes.description: Internet usage to source information.

performance_criteria.description: Identify sources of internet.
Understand and explain how to use internet to source information.
Identify websites relevant to your field of operation.

learning_outcomes.description: Organize mixing for adhesives for Tile work.

performance_criteria.description: Organize to request for materials for mixing adhesives.
Organize to prepare position of mixing adhesives according to mechanical or manual methods.
Organize to measure the materials.
Supervise the mixing of adhesive according to requirements.

learning_outcomes.description: Supervise the laying of tiles to level and alignment.

performance_criteria.description: Provide any suitable working drawing e.g. Residential, Commercial or Educational.
Interpret the working drawing to locate area to be tiled.
Organize to set out the area to be tiled as usual.
Organize to select the tiles required according to size and color.
Supervise the cutting of any fraction of size using diamond cutter or mechanical tile cutter.
Supervise the soaking of tiles to absorb initial water so as to prevent tiles from absorbing water from the mortar.
Organize to measure materials for adhesive as required.
Supervise the mixing of adhesive on selected and prepared area.
Supervise the laying of tiles to standard using the adhesives.
Confirm tiles are laid, leveled and aligned including all fractions.
Monitor the laying of the tiles to the final level. g 3.2 Organize to grout the joints using adhesives.
Supervise the cleaning of tiled surface and work area.
Inspect tile joints as work proceed to ensure bond and joint alignment.
Supervise the use of line, straight edge and level as work proceed to ensure they are correctly used.
Certify each joint line before proceeding to the level.
Confirm and supervise tile work for wall and floor as required to the final point.
Organize to grout the joint.

learning_outcomes.description: Organise and supervise the fixing and laying mosaic tiles to the background.

performance_criteria.description: Organize to measure area to receive mosaic.
Organize to select the color and pattern of mosaic required.
Calculate the area of mosaic.
Order mosaic with required fixing adhesive.
Organize and supervise the fixing of mosaic in position.
Ensure joints are filled.
Ensure the selected pattern is the designed pattern.
Organize order for required materials.
Fix mosaic to required position.
Ensure it is hard, aligned and truly flat on wall or floor.
Confirm joint alignment as straight.
Confirm joint width are uniform.
Organize to fill joints width grout, i.e. slurry or thick cement liquid.
Organize to lean excess grout on mosaic or tiles.
Assess general work produced to confirm job is clean according to requirement.

learning_outcomes.description: Organize and supervise tile work on curved surfaces.

performance_criteria.description: Inspect the working drawing to locate position of curved work.
Inspect the work to locate the position physically as detailed on the working.
Organize to ensure tiles are prepared for the position.
Ensure quantity of tiles required is estimated.

learning_outcomes.description: Prepare and fix tiles

performance_criteria.description: Organize to set out the curved surfaces by checking the layout of the tiles.
Organize to mark the position of tiles on the curve.
Prepare and organize cutting of the tiles if so required.
Supervise the fixing of tiles using approved mortar.
Check to see that curve is uniform according to requirement.
Confirm accuracy of work.
Request for provision of scaffold for all the tile work at higher level in accordance with rules and regulations concerning working place.
